text: If I had known, I would have booked earlier.
If I had taken that trip, I would have learned the language.
I wish I had learned English sooner.
If only I had taken that trip.
Do you ever look back on that summer we didn't go to Portugal?
All the time. If we had gone, we would have met your cousin in Lisbon.
In hindsight, I wish I had been a little braver back then.
Maybe. But if we had gone, we would never have taken the road trip we love now.
That's true. It all came full circle, didn't it? No regrets, then.
No regrets. Let's just make the most of the next one.
In hindsight, it was a real turning point.
I try not to dwell on it -- no regrets.
Let's make the most of the road ahead.

There's one trip I still think about, even now. Years ago, I had the chance to spend a whole month travelling through the south of Italy, slowly, with no fixed plan. But I was nervous about being away from work for so long, so I cut it down to ten days and rushed through everything. In hindsight, I wish I had trusted myself more. If I had taken the full month, I would have learned the language properly, I would have made real friends, and I would have come home a different person. If only I had known then what I know now. And yet, I try not to dwell on it. That short trip was still a turning point. It taught me that I love to travel slowly, and it gave me the courage to plan the long journeys I take today. So when I look back on it, the regret is real, but it is gentle. If I had not made that mistake, I would not have understood what truly matters to me. In a strange way, it all came full circle.

Hey Pricila, it's me. I got your message about looking back on the old days, and I wanted to leave you a quick thought. You asked if I have any regrets about all the trips we never took. Honestly? Not really. Sure, if I had been braver when I was younger, I would have seen more of the world by now. If I had saved a little more, I would have gone to Japan when we first talked about it twenty years ago. But here is the thing: I live by no regrets. Every choice I made, good or bad, brought me to a life I am happy with. I'd rather make the most of the road ahead than second-guess the road behind me. So my advice, traveller to traveller, is this: take stock of what you have learned, but don't dwell on the doors you didn't open. Look back just long enough to smile, and then look forward. The best journey is always the next one. Talk soon.

First, Pricila looks back on her short trip to Italy. In hindsight, she wishes she had stayed the full month. Then she thinks: if she had stayed, she would have learned the language. After that, she realizes it was a turning point that shaped her life. So she lets it go with no regrets and looks forward.
If I had known, I would have booked earlier.
I wish I had learned English sooner.
If only I had taken that trip.
If we had saved, we could have gone to Japan.
If I hadn't made that mistake, I wouldn't have arrived here.
In hindsight, it was a real turning point.
Let's make the most of the road ahead.
